label: hypercatalectic
gloss: extra syllable or syllables at the end of a metrical foot

label: catalectic
gloss: metrically incomplete line of verse, lacking a syllable at the end or ending with an incomplete foot

label: acatalectic
gloss: metrically complete number of syllables in the final foot

label: brachycatalectic
gloss: omission of two syllables at the end of a verse composed of the larger metrical units

label: anacrustic
gloss: unstressed syllable or syllable group that begins a line of verse but is not counted as part of the first foot

label: acephalous
gloss: metrically incomplete line of verse, lacking a syllable at the start or starting with an incomplete foot

label: broken
gloss: broken over line end, e.g. .../king-/ / /Dom/

label: split
gloss: using more than one word, e.g. .../Minute/ / .../in it/

label: linked
gloss: using enjambement

label: apocopated
gloss: parts of words rhyme, e.g. .../feel/ / .../real/ly

label: Italian (Petrarchan) sonnet
gloss: abba abba cde cde

label: georgic
gloss: a poem or book dealing with agriculture or rural topics.

label: Chevy Chase stanza
gloss: alternating lines of iambic tetrameter and pentameter, in quatrains

label: epigram
gloss: a short poem, especially a satirical one, with a witty or ingenious ending

label: eulogy
gloss: praise of a recently deceased

label: panegyric
gloss: praise of a person

label: monody
gloss: lament

label: distich
gloss: couplet

label: epitaph
gloss: a phrase or form of words written in memory of a person who has died, especially as an inscription on a tombstone

label: lampoon
gloss: publicly criticize (someone or something) by using ridicule, irony, or sarcasm

label: pastoral
gloss: a work of literature portraying an idealized version of country life

label: epithalamion
gloss: song or poem to the bride and bridegroom at their wedding

label: anacreontic
gloss: a poem written in the style of the ancient Greek poet Anacreon, especially one celebrating love and wine

label: eclogue
gloss: a short poem, especially a pastoral dialogue

label: burlesque
gloss: intended to cause laughter by caricaturing the manner or spirit of serious works, or by ludicrous treatment of their subjects

label: cento
gloss: a poetical work wholly composed of verses or passages taken from other authors disposed in a new form or order

label: allegory
gloss: An extended metaphor in which the characters, places, and objects in a narrative carry figurative meaning
